title: >= 3 annotated walkthroughs across study types (licence-verified OA)
context: The M2 toolkit is made concrete by annotated walkthroughs across different study types. Three or more walkthroughs are required, each using a distinct, licence-verified, fully open-access oncology paper and applying the matching worksheet inline. Each paper must be individually verified for a reuse-permitting licence before authoring begins. Reviewers with a COI on a specific paper must recuse from that walkthrough.
objective: Author three or more annotated walkthroughs of distinct licence-verified, fully open-access oncology papers — one per study type (observational, early-phase/single-arm, systematic review, or additional RCT) — each applying the matching worksheet inline and obtaining dual high-tier sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: At least three walkthroughs, each using a distinct oncology paper with a verified reuse-permitting licence (CC0, CC-BY, or CC-BY-SA) recorded in data/sources.yaml with PMCID, DOI, licence, licenceVerifiedDate, and verifiedBy before authoring begins.

Walkthroughs span at least three distinct study types: at minimum one observational/cohort study, one early-phase or single-arm study, and one systematic review or meta-analysis (in addition to the RCT walkthrough from task 008).

Each walkthrough applies the matching worksheet (RCT worksheet 007, early-phase worksheet 201, observational worksheet 202, or SR/MA worksheet 203) inline, addressing each worksheet question with appraisal commentary on the specific paper.
Figures reproduced only if the paper's specific licence explicitly permits reproduction; otherwise linked by PMCID/figure number.
Renders no verdicts on the papers' findings or on the drugs/treatments studied; framing is purely methodological.
Carries a persistent 'education only — not medical advice' notice; passes the advice-language checklist (task 002).
Any reviewer with a material conflict of interest on a specific paper recuses from that paper's sign-off, with the recusal recorded in the review ledger.
Passes the licence + provenance build gate and CI for all three walkthroughs.
Each walkthrough is signed off by a credentialed oncologist (recorded in the review ledger): clinical framing correct, nothing reads as treatment guidance.
Each walkthrough is signed off by an experienced patient advocate (recorded in the review ledger): clear, accessible, does not stray into advice.
Each walkthrough is signed off by a methodology reviewer: appraisal commentary is methodologically accurate for the given study type.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Scope — annotated walkthroughs; § Data, licensing & compliance — Source classes)
output: Three or more annotated walkthrough documents (content/walkthroughs/) committed to the repo, each covering a distinct study type with worksheet inline, sources.yaml entries for all papers, CI-green, and carrying dual high-tier sign-off plus methodology review for each walkthrough.
